#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""

AGI Hivemind Chatbot - Main Entry Point



A command-line AGI hivemind chatbot system with multiple collaborative AI agents.

"""



import os

import sys

import click

from rich.console import Console

from rich.panel import Panel

from rich.text import Text



from cli.interface import ChatInterface

from hivemind.coordinator import HivemindCoordinator

from utils.logger import setup_logger



console = Console()

logger = setup_logger()



@click.group()

@click.option('--debug', is_flag=True, help='Enable debug mode')

@click.pass_context

def cli(ctx, debug):

"""AGI Hivemind Chatbot - A collaborative AI agent system"""

ctx.ensure_object(dict)

ctx.obj['debug'] = debug


if debug:

logger.setLevel('DEBUG')



@cli.command()

@click.option('--agents', '-a', multiple=True,

help='Specify which agents to activate (analyst, creative, technical)')

@click.pass_context

def chat(ctx, agents):

"""Start an interactive chat session with the hivemind"""


# Welcome message

welcome_text = Text()

welcome_text.append("🧠 AGI Hivemind Chatbot\n", style="bold blue")

welcome_text.append("Collaborative AI Agent System", style="dim")


console.print(Panel(welcome_text, title="Welcome"))


try:

# Initialize the hivemind coordinator

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator(debug=ctx.obj.get('debug', False))


# Activate specified agents or all by default

if agents:

coordinator.activate_agents(list(agents))

else:

coordinator.activate_all_agents()


# Start the chat interface

chat_interface = ChatInterface(coordinator, console)

chat_interface.start()


except KeyboardInterrupt:

console.print("\n[yellow]Chat session ended by user.[/yellow]")

except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

if ctx.obj.get('debug'):

import traceback

console.print(traceback.format_exc())

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

def agents():

"""List available agents and their capabilities"""


try:

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator()

agent_info = coordinator.get_agent_info()


console.print(Panel("Available AI Agents", style="bold blue"))


for agent_name, info in agent_info.items():

agent_panel = Panel(

f"[bold]{info['description']}[/bold]\n\n"

f"Capabilities:\n{info['capabilities']}\n\n"

f"Personality: {info['personality']}",

title=f"🤖 {agent_name.title()}",

border_style="green"

)

console.print(agent_panel)

console.print()


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

@click.option('--clear', is_flag=True, help='Clear all conversation history')

def history(clear):

"""Manage conversation history"""


from storage.history import ConversationHistory


try:

hist = ConversationHistory()


if clear:

hist.clear_all()

console.print("[green]✓ All conversation history cleared.[/green]")

else:

sessions = hist.list_sessions()

if sessions:

console.print(Panel("Recent Conversation Sessions", style="bold blue"))

for session in sessions[-10:]: # Show last 10 sessions

console.print(f"• {session['timestamp']} - {session['message_count']} messages")

else:

console.print("[dim]No conversation history found.[/dim]")


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)



if __name__ == '__main__':

cli()"""

Base Agent Class



Defines the common interface and functionality for all AI agents in the hivemind.

"""



import json

import os

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

import os

import random

from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any

from utils.logger import get_logger

from tools.toolkit import ToolKit, TaskHandler, SharedMemory



logger = get_logger(__name__)



class BaseAgent(ABC):

"""Abstract base class for all AI agents"""


def __init__(self, name: str, personality: str, capabilities: List[str],

toolkit: Optional[ToolKit] = None, shared_memory: Optional[SharedMemory] = None):

self.name = name

self.personality = personality

self.capabilities = capabilities

self.memory = [] # Conversation memory

self.context = {} # Shared context with other agents


# Initialize tool system

self.toolkit = toolkit or ToolKit()

self.task_handler = TaskHandler(self.toolkit)

self.shared_memory = shared_memory


# Initialize local AI response system

self.response_templates = self._initialize_response_templates()


logger.info(f"Initialized agent: {self.name} with tools: {len(self.toolkit.list_tools())}")


@abstractmethod

def get_system_prompt(self) -> str:

"""Return the system prompt that defines this agent's behavior"""

pass


@abstractmethod

def get_specialization(self) -> str:

"""Return a description of this agent's specialization"""

pass


async def generate_response(self, user_message: str, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> str:

"""Generate a response to the user's message"""

try:

# Check for special commands first

command_response = self.task_handler.handle_command(user_message)

if command_response:

if command_response == "CLEAR_CONTEXT":

self.clear_memory()

return "I've cleared my memory and we're starting fresh!"

return command_response


# Generate local AI response

agent_response = self._generate_local_response(user_message, context)


# Store in memory

self.memory.append([

{"role": "user", "content": user_message},

{"role": "assistant", "content": agent_response}

])


# Store in shared memory if available

if self.shared_memory:

self.shared_memory.add_memory(

self.name,

f"Responded to: {user_message[:50]}... with: {agent_response[:100]}...",

context=user_message,

importance=2 if len(agent_response) > 200 else 1

)


# Keep memory manageable

if len(self.memory) > 10:

self.memory = self.memory[-10:]


logger.debug(f"{self.name} generated response for: {user_message[:50]}...")

return agent_response


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error generating response for {self.name}: {str(e)}")

return f"I apologize, but I'm having trouble processing your request right now. Error: {str(e)}"


async def consult_on_topic(self, topic: str, query_context: str = "") -> str:

"""Provide a brief consultation on a specific topic for other agents"""

try:

# Generate local consultation response

consultation = self._generate_consultation_response(topic, query_context)

logger.debug(f"{self.name} provided consultation on: {topic}")

return consultation


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error in consultation for {self.name}: {str(e)}")

return f"Unable to provide consultation at this time."


def _generate_local_response(self, user_message: str, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> str:

"""Generate a local AI response without external API"""

try:

# Simple keyword-based response generation

message_lower = user_message.lower().strip()


# Handle greetings

if any(greeting in message_lower for greeting in ['hello', 'hi', 'hey', 'greetings']):

template = random.choice(self.response_templates["greetings"])

return template.format(specialization=self.get_specialization())


# Handle farewells

if any(farewell in message_lower for farewell in ['bye', 'goodbye', 'thanks', 'thank you']):

return random.choice(self.response_templates["farewells"])


# Handle questions about capabilities

if 'what can you do' in message_lower or 'help' in message_lower:

capabilities_text = ", ".join(self.capabilities)

return f"I specialize in {self.get_specialization()}. My capabilities include: {capabilities_text}. I also have access to these tools: {', '.join(self.toolkit.list_tools())}. You can use commands like !calc, !code, !time, !read, !write to access them."


# Generate contextual response based on agent type

response_content = self._generate_contextual_response(user_message)


return response_content


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error in local response generation: {str(e)}")

return f"I apologize, but I'm having trouble processing your request right now. As a {self.get_specialization()}, I'm here to help with {', '.join(self.capabilities[:3])} and similar tasks."


def _generate_contextual_response(self, user_message: str) -> str:

"""Generate response based on agent specialization - to be overridden by subclasses"""

return f"I understand you're asking about: '{user_message}'. As a {self.personality} {self.get_specialization()}, I'd be happy to help you with this topic using my expertise in {', '.join(self.capabilities[:2])}."


def _generate_consultation_response(self, topic: str, query_context: str = "") -> str:

"""Generate a consultation response for other agents"""

try:

context_info = f" considering the context: {query_context}" if query_context else ""

return f"From my {self.get_specialization()} perspective on '{topic}'{context_info}, I recommend focusing on {random.choice(self.capabilities)}. This aligns with my {self.personality} approach to problem-solving."

except Exception:

return f"As a {self.get_specialization()}, I can provide insights on {topic} based on my expertise."


def _initialize_response_templates(self):

"""Initialize response templates for local AI generation"""

return {

"greetings": [

"Hello! I'm ready to help you with my {specialization} expertise.",

"Hi there! How can I assist you today?",

"Welcome! I'm here to help with {specialization} tasks."

],

"general": [

"Based on my {specialization} background, I think {response}.",

"From my perspective as a {personality} agent, {response}.",

"Here's my take on this: {response}"

],

"farewells": [

"Happy to help! Let me know if you need anything else.",

"Hope that was useful! Feel free to ask more questions.",

"Glad I could assist you today!"

]

}


def update_context(self, context: Dict[str, Any]):

"""Update shared context information"""

self.context.update(context)


def clear_memory(self):

"""Clear the agent's conversation memory"""

self.memory = []

logger.info(f"Cleared memory for agent: {self.name}")


def get_info(self) -> Dict[str, str]:

"""Return information about this agent"""

return {

"name": self.name,

"personality": self.personality,

"capabilities": ", ".join(self.capabilities),

"specialization": self.get_specialization(),

"description": self.get_system_prompt().split('.')[0] + ".",

"tools": ", ".join(self.toolkit.list_tools())

}"""

Analyst Agent



Specializes in data analysis, logical reasoning, and systematic problem-solving.

"""



from typing import List, Optional

from storage.agents.base_agent import BaseAgent

from tools.toolkit import ToolKit, SharedMemory



class AnalystAgent(BaseAgent):

"""Agent specialized in analysis, logic, and systematic thinking"""


def __init__(self, toolkit: Optional[ToolKit] = None, shared_memory: Optional[SharedMemory] = None):

super().__init__(

name="analyst",

personality="logical, methodical, data-driven",

capabilities=[

"data analysis",

"logical reasoning",

"problem decomposition",

"pattern recognition",

"statistical thinking",

"research methodology"

],

toolkit=toolkit,

shared_memory=shared_memory

)


def get_system_prompt(self) -> str:

return """You are the Analyst agent in an AGI hivemind system. Your role is to provide logical, data-driven, and systematic analysis of problems and questions.



Key characteristics:

- Approach problems methodically and systematically

- Break down complex issues into manageable components

- Look for patterns, trends, and underlying structures

- Use evidence-based reasoning

- Provide clear, logical explanations

- Ask clarifying questions when information is incomplete

- Suggest data collection or research when needed



Your personality is professional, precise, and intellectually curious. You value accuracy and thoroughness over speed. When collaborating with other agents in the hivemind, you provide the analytical foundation and logical framework for decision-making.



Always structure your responses clearly with logical flow and support your conclusions with reasoning."""


def get_specialization(self) -> str:

return "systematic analyst focused on logical reasoning and data-driven insights"


def _generate_contextual_response(self, user_message: str) -> str:

"""Generate analyst-specific responses with logical structure"""

message_lower = user_message.lower()


# Analysis keywords

if any(keyword in message_lower for keyword in ['analyze', 'data', 'statistics', 'research', 'study']):

return f"From an analytical perspective, let me break down '{user_message}': I'd recommend approaching this systematically by first gathering relevant data, then identifying key patterns and variables, and finally drawing evidence-based conclusions. What specific data points would you like me to help you examine?"


# Problem-solving keywords

elif any(keyword in message_lower for keyword in ['problem', 'issue', 'solve', 'solution', 'fix']):

return f"I'll approach this problem methodically: '{user_message}'. Let me decompose this into manageable components. First, we need to identify the root cause, then evaluate potential solutions based on data and evidence. What additional information do you have about this issue?"


# General analytical response

else:

return f"As your analytical agent, I'll examine '{user_message}' through a logical lens. Based on my systematic approach, I recommend we gather more context and data to ensure our conclusions are well-founded. What specific aspects would you like me to analyze further?""""

Creative Agent



Specializes in creative thinking, innovation, and generating novel solutions.

"""



from typing import List, Optional

from storage.agents.base_agent import BaseAgent

from tools.toolkit import ToolKit, SharedMemory



class CreativeAgent(BaseAgent):

"""Agent specialized in creative thinking and innovative solutions"""


def __init__(self, toolkit: Optional[ToolKit] = None, shared_memory: Optional[SharedMemory] = None):

super().__init__(

name="creative",

personality="imaginative, innovative, expressive",

capabilities=[

"creative problem solving",

"brainstorming",

"lateral thinking",

"storytelling",

"design thinking",

"artistic expression",

"innovative solutions"

],

toolkit=toolkit,

shared_memory=shared_memory

)


def get_system_prompt(self) -> str:

return """You are the Creative agent in an AGI hivemind system. Your role is to bring innovation, imagination, and novel perspectives to problems and discussions.



Key characteristics:

- Think outside the box and challenge conventional approaches

- Generate multiple creative alternatives and possibilities

- Use analogies, metaphors, and storytelling to illustrate points

- Embrace experimentation and novel ideas

- Look for unexpected connections and opportunities

- Encourage exploration of "what if" scenarios

- Balance creativity with practical considerations



Your personality is enthusiastic, imaginative, and optimistic. You see possibilities where others might see obstacles. When collaborating with other agents in the hivemind, you provide the spark of innovation and help expand thinking beyond conventional boundaries.



Express yourself with enthusiasm and use vivid language to paint pictures of possibilities. Don't be afraid to suggest bold or unconventional ideas."""


def get_specialization("""

Tool System

Enhanced tool capabilities for AGI agents inspired by the goddess system.

"""



import subprocess

import sys

import time

import json

import os

import sqlite3

import threading

from typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List

from datetime import datetime

from utils.logger import get_logger



logger = get_logger(__name__)



class ToolKit:

"""A collection of tools that agents can use to interact with the world."""


def __init__(self):

self.tools = {

'python_interpreter': self.python_interpreter,

'file_reader': self.file_reader,

'file_writer': self.file_writer,

'calculator': self.calculator,

'time_info': self.time_info,

'system_info': self.system_info

}

logger.info("ToolKit initialized with available tools")


def python_interpreter(self, code: str) -> str:

"""Executes Python code in a sandboxed environment and returns the output."""

logger.info(f"Executing Python code: {code[:100]}...")

try:

# Basic security check - prevent dangerous operations

dangerous_imports = ['os', 'subprocess', 'sys', '__import__', 'eval', 'exec']

for danger in dangerous_imports:

if danger in code.lower():

return f"Security restriction: '{danger}' operations are not allowed."


result = subprocess.run(

[sys.executable, "-c", code],

capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10, check=True

)

output = result.stdout

if result.stderr:

output += "\nSTDERR:\n" + result.stderr

return output.strip() or "Code executed successfully (no output)"

except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:

return "Error: Code execution timed out after 10 seconds."

except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:

return f"Error executing code: {e}\nOutput:\n{e.stdout}{e.stderr}"

except Exception as e:

return f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}"


def file_reader(self, filename: str) -> str:

"""Reads content from a file (limited to safe directories)."""

logger.info(f"Reading file: {filename}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow reading from current directory and subdirectories

if '..' in filename or filename.startswith('/'):

return "Security restriction: Only files in current directory are accessible."


if not os.path.exists(filename):

return f"Error: File '{filename}' not found."


with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:

content = f.read()

if len(content) > 5000:

content = content[:5000] + "\n... (file truncated at 5000 characters)"

return content

except Exception as e:

return f"Error reading file: {e}"


def file_writer(self, data: Dict[str, str]) -> str:

"""Writes content to a file (limited to safe directories)."""

filename = data.get('filename')

content = data.get('content')


if not filename or not content:

return "Error: Both 'filename' and 'content' must be provided."


logger.info(f"Writing to file: {filename}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow writing to current directory and subdirectories

if '..' in filename or filename.startswith('/'):

return "Security restriction: Only files in current directory are accessible."


with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:

f.write(content)

return f"Successfully wrote {len(content)} characters to '{filename}'"

except Exception as e:

return f"Error writing file: {e}"


def calculator(self, expression: str) -> str:

"""Performs safe mathematical calculations."""

logger.info(f"Calculating: {expression}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow mathematical operations

allowed_chars = set('0123456789+-*/.() ')

if not all(c in allowed_chars for c in expression):

return "Error: Only basic mathematical operations are allowed."


result = eval(expression, {"__builtins__": {}})

return f"Result: {result}"

except Exception as e:

return f"Calculation error: {e}"


def time_info(self, format_type: str = "full") -> str:

"""Returns current time information."""

logger.info(f"Getting time info: {format_type}")

try:

now = datetime.now()

if format_type == "time":

return now.strftime("%H:%M:%S")

elif format_type == "date":

return now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

elif format_type == "timestamp":

return str(int(now.timestamp()))

else:

return now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

except Exception as e:

return f"Error getting time: {e}"


def system_info(self, info_type: str = "all") -> str:

"""Returns system information."""

logger.info(f"Getting system info: {info_type}")

try:

info = {

"platform": sys.platform,

"python_version": sys.version.split()[0],

"current_directory": os.getcwd()

}


if info_type == "all":

return json.dumps(info, indent=2)

elif info_type in info:

return str(info[info_type])

else:

return f"Available info types: {list(info.keys())}"

except Exception as e:

return f"Error getting system info: {e}"


def execute_tool(self, tool_name: str, tool_input: Any) -> str:

"""Execute a specific tool with input."""

if tool_name not in self.tools:

return f"Error: Tool '{tool_name}' not found. Available tools: {list(self.tools.keys())}"


try:

return self.tools[tool_name](tool_input)

except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error executing tool {tool_name}: {str(e)}")

return f"Tool execution error: {str(e)}"


def list_tools(self) -> List[str]:

"""List all available tools."""

return list(self.tools.keys())





class TaskHandler:

"""Enhanced task handling system inspired by aether_jarvis."""


def __init__(self, toolkit: ToolKit):

self.toolkit = toolkit

self.commands = {

'!help': self._show_help,

'!tools': self._list_tools,

'!time': self._get_time,

'!calc': self._calculate,

'!code': self._execute_code,

'!read': self._read_file,

'!write': self._write_file,

'!system': self._system_info,

'!clear': self._clear_context

}

logger.info("TaskHandler initialized with enhanced commands")


def handle_command(self, command: str) -> Optional[str]:

"""Handle special commands that start with !"""

if not command.startswith('!'):

return None


parts = command.split(maxsplit=1)

cmd = parts[0].lower()

args = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""


if cmd in self.commands:

return self.commands[cmd](args)

else:

return f"Unknown command: {cmd}. Type !help for available commands."


def _show_help(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Show available commands."""

return """Available Commands:

!help - Show this help message

!tools - List available tools

!time [format] - Get current time (formats: time, date, timestamp, full)

!calc <expression> - Perform mathematical calculation

!code <python_code> - Execute Python code safely

!read <filename> - Read content from a file

!write <filename> <content> - Write content to a file

!system [info_type] - Get system information

!clear - Clear conversation context



Example: !calc 2 + 2 * 3

Example: !code print("Hello, World!")

Example: !time date"""1


def _list_tools(self, args: str) -> str:

"""List available tools."""

tools = self.toolkit.list_tools()

return f"Available tools: {', '.join(tools)}"


def _get_time(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Get current time."""

format_type = args.strip() or "full"

return self.toolkit.time_info(format_type)


def _calculate(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Perform calculation."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide a mathematical expression. Example: !calc 2 + 2"

return self.toolkit.calculator(args.strip())


def _execute_code(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Execute Python code."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide Python code to execute. Example: !code print('Hello')"

return self.toolkit.python_interpreter(args.strip())


def _read_file(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Read a file."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide a filename. Example: !read config.txt"

return self.toolkit.file_reader(args.strip())


def _write_file(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Write to a file."""

parts = args.split(maxsplit=1)

if len(parts) < 2:

return "Please provide filename and content. Example: !write test.txt Hello World"


filename = parts[0]

content = parts[1]

return self.toolkit.file_writer({"filename": filename, "content": content})


def _system_info(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Get system information."""

info_type = args.strip() or "all"

return self.toolkit.system_info(info_type)


def _clear_context(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Clear conversation context."""

return "CLEAR_CONTEXT"





class SharedMemory:

"""Enhanced shared memory system with SQLite backend."""


def __init__(self, db_path: str = "hivemind_memory.db"):

self.db_path = db_path

self._lock = threading.Lock()

self._setup_database()

logger.info(f"SharedMemory initialized at {db_path}")


def _setup_database(self):

"""Initialize the database schema."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


cursor.execute('''

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memories (

id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,

agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,

timestamp REAL NOT NULL,

content TEXT NOT NULL,

context TEXT,

importance INTEGER DEFAULT 1

)

''')


cursor.execute('''

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS interactions (

id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,

session_id TEXT NOT NULL,

timestamp REAL NOT NULL,

user_input TEXT NOT NULL,

agent_responses TEXT NOT NULL

)

''')


conn.commit()

conn.close()


def add_memory(self, agent_id: str, content: str, context: str = "", importance: int = 1):

"""Add a memory entry."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


cursor.execute(

"INSERT INTO memories (agent_id, timestamp, content, context, importance) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",

(agent_id, time.time(), content, context, importance)

)


conn.commit()

conn.close()


logger.debug(f"Memory added from {agent_id}: {content[:50]}...")


def recall_memories(self, agent_id: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:

"""Recall recent memories."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


if agent_id is not None:

cursor.execute(

"SELECT agent_id, content, context, importance, timestamp FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ? ORDER BY importance DESC, timestamp DESC LIMIT ?",

(agent_id, limit)

)

else:

cursor.execute(

"SELECT agent_id, content, context, importance, timestamp FROM memories ORDER BY importance DESC, timestamp DESC LIMIT ?",

(limit,)

)


memories = cursor.fetchall()

conn.close()


return [

{

"agent_id": mem[0],

"content": mem[1],

"context": mem[2],

"importance": mem[3],

"timestamp": mem[4]

}

for mem in memories

]


def clear_memories(self, agent_id: Optional[str] = None):

"""Clear memories for a specific agent or all agents."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


if agent_id is not None:

cursor.execute("DELETE FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ?", (agent_id,))

else:

cursor.execute("DELETE FROM memories")


conn.commit()

conn.close()


logger.info(f"Cleared memories for {agent_id if agent_id is not None else 'all agents'}")#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""

AGI Hivemind Chatbot - Main Entry Point



A command-line AGI hivemind chatbot system with multiple collaborative AI agents.

"""



import os

import sys

import click

from rich.console import Console

from rich.panel import Panel

from rich.text import Text



from cli.interface import ChatInterface

from hivemind.coordinator import HivemindCoordinator

from utils.logger import setup_logger



console = Console()

logger = setup_logger()



@click.group()

@click.option('--debug', is_flag=True, help='Enable debug mode')

@click.pass_context

def cli(ctx, debug):

"""AGI Hivemind Chatbot - A collaborative AI agent system"""

ctx.ensure_object(dict)

ctx.obj['debug'] = debug


if debug:

logger.setLevel('DEBUG')



@cli.command()

@click.option('--agents', '-a', multiple=True,

help='Specify which agents to activate (analyst, creative, technical)')

@click.pass_context

def chat(ctx, agents):

"""Start an interactive chat session with the hivemind"""


# Welcome message

welcome_text = Text()

welcome_text.append("🧠 AGI Hivemind Chatbot\n", style="bold blue")

welcome_text.append("Collaborative AI Agent System", style="dim")


console.print(Panel(welcome_text, title="Welcome"))


try:

# Initialize the hivemind coordinator

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator(debug=ctx.obj.get('debug', False))


# Activate specified agents or all by default

if agents:

coordinator.activate_agents(list(agents))

else:

coordinator.activate_all_agents()


# Start the chat interface

chat_interface = ChatInterface(coordinator, console)

chat_interface.start()


except KeyboardInterrupt:

console.print("\n[yellow]Chat session ended by user.[/yellow]")

except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

if ctx.obj.get('debug'):

import traceback

console.print(traceback.format_exc())

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

def agents():

"""List available agents and their capabilities"""


try:

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator()

agent_info = coordinator.get_agent_info()


console.print(Panel("Available AI Agents", style="bold blue"))


for agent_name, info in agent_info.items():

agent_panel = Panel(

f"[bold]{info['description']}[/bold]\n\n"

f"Capabilities:\n{info['capabilities']}\n\n"

f"Personality: {info['personality']}",

title=f"🤖 {agent_name.title()}",

border_style="green"

)

console.print(agent_panel)

console.print()


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

@click.option('--clear', is_flag=True, help='Clear all conversation history')

def history(clear):

"""Manage conversation history"""


from storage.history import ConversationHistory


try:

hist = ConversationHistory()


if clear:

hist.clear_all()

console.print("[green]✓ All conversation history cleared.[/green]")

else:

sessions = hist.list_sessions()

if sessions:

console.print(Panel("Recent Conversation Sessions", style="bold blue"))

for session in sessions[-10:]: # Show last 10 sessions

console.print(f"• {session['timestamp']} - {session['message_count']} messages")

else:

console.print("[dim]No conversation history found.[/dim]")


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)



if __name__ == '__main__':

cli()#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""

AGI Hivemind Chatbot - Main Entry Point



A command-line AGI hivemind chatbot system with multiple collaborative AI agents.

"""



import os

import sys

import click

from rich.console import Console

from rich.panel import Panel

from rich.text import Text



from cli.interface import ChatInterface

from hivemind.coordinator import HivemindCoordinator

from utils.logger import setup_logger



console = Console()

logger = setup_logger()



@click.group()

@click.option('--debug', is_flag=True, help='Enable debug mode')

@click.pass_context

def cli(ctx, debug):

"""AGI Hivemind Chatbot - A collaborative AI agent system"""

ctx.ensure_object(dict)

ctx.obj['debug'] = debug


if debug:

logger.setLevel('DEBUG')



@cli.command()

@click.option('--agents', '-a', multiple=True,

help='Specify which agents to activate (analyst, creative, technical)')

@click.pass_context

def chat(ctx, agents):

"""Start an interactive chat session with the hivemind"""


# Welcome message

welcome_text = Text()

welcome_text.append("🧠 AGI Hivemind Chatbot\n", style="bold blue")

welcome_text.append("Collaborative AI Agent System", style="dim")


console.print(Panel(welcome_text, title="Welcome"))


try:

# Initialize the hivemind coordinator

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator(debug=ctx.obj.get('debug', False))


# Activate specified agents or all by default

if agents:

coordinator.activate_agents(list(agents))

else:

coordinator.activate_all_agents()


# Start the chat interface

chat_interface = ChatInterface(coordinator, console)

chat_interface.start()


except KeyboardInterrupt:

console.print("\n[yellow]Chat session ended by user.[/yellow]")

except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

if ctx.obj.get('debug'):

import traceback

console.print(traceback.format_exc())

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

def agents():

"""List available agents and their capabilities"""


try:

coordinator = HivemindCoordinator()

agent_info = coordinator.get_agent_info()


console.print(Panel("Available AI Agents", style="bold blue"))


for agent_name, info in agent_info.items():

agent_panel = Panel(

f"[bold]{info['description']}[/bold]\n\n"

f"Capabilities:\n{info['capabilities']}\n\n"

f"Personality: {info['personality']}",

title=f"🤖 {agent_name.title()}",

border_style="green"

)

console.print(agent_panel)

console.print()


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)



@cli.command()

@click.option('--clear', is_flag=True, help='Clear all conversation history')

def history(clear):

"""Manage conversation history"""


from storage.history import ConversationHistory


try:

hist = ConversationHistory()


if clear:

hist.clear_all()

console.print("[green]✓ All conversation history cleared.[/green]")

else:

sessions = hist.list_sessions()

if sessions:

console.print(Panel("Recent Conversation Sessions", style="bold blue"))

for session in sessions[-10:]: # Show last 10 sessions

console.print(f"• {session['timestamp']} - {session['message_count']} messages")

else:

console.print("[dim]No conversation history found.[/dim]")


except Exception as e:

console.print(f"[red]Error: {str(e)}[/red]")

sys.exit(1)

cli()"""

Base Agent Class



Defines the common interface and functionality for all AI agents in the hivemind.

"""



import json

import os

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

import os

import random

from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Any

from utils.logger import get_logger

from tools.toolkit import ToolKit, TaskHandler, SharedMemory



logger = get_logger(__name__)



class BaseAgent(ABC):

"""Abstract base class for all AI agents"""


def __init__(self, name: str, personality: str, capabilities: List[str],

toolkit: Optional[ToolKit] = None, shared_memory: Optional[SharedMemory] = None):

self.name = name

self.personality = personality

self.capabilities = capabilities

self.memory = [] # Conversation memory

self.context = {} # Shared context with other agents


# Initialize tool system

self.toolkit = toolkit or ToolKit()

self.task_handler = TaskHandler(self.toolkit)

self.shared_memory = shared_memory


# Initialize local AI response system

self.response_templates = self._initialize_response_templates()


logger.info(f"Initialized agent: {self.name} with tools: {len(self.toolkit.list_tools())}")


@abstractmethod

def get_system_prompt(self) -> str:

"""Return the system prompt that defines this agent's behavior"""

pass


@abstractmethod

def get_specialization(self) -> str:

"""Return a description of this agent's specialization"""

pass


async def generate_response(self, user_message: str, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> str:

"""Generate a response to the user's message"""

try:

# Check for special commands first

command_response = self.task_handler.handle_command(user_message)

if command_response:

if command_response == "CLEAR_CONTEXT":

self.clear_memory()

return "I've cleared my memory and we're starting fresh!"

return command_response


# Generate local AI response

agent_response = self._generate_local_response(user_message, context)


# Store in memory

self.memory.append([

{"role": "user", "content": user_message},

{"role": "assistant", "content": agent_response}

])


# Store in shared memory if available

if self.shared_memory:

self.shared_memory.add_memory(

self.name,

f"Responded to: {user_message[:50]}... with: {agent_response[:100]}...",

context=user_message,

importance=2 if len(agent_response) > 200 else 1

)


# Keep memory manageable

if len(self.memory) > 10:

self.memory = self.memory[-10:]


logger.debug(f"{self.name} generated response for: {user_message[:50]}...")

return agent_response


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error generating response for {self.name}: {str(e)}")

return f"I apologize, but I'm having trouble processing your request right now. Error: {str(e)}"


async def consult_on_topic(self, topic: str, query_context: str = "") -> str:

"""Provide a brief consultation on a specific topic for other agents"""

try:

# Generate local consultation response

consultation = self._generate_consultation_response(topic, query_context)

logger.debug(f"{self.name} provided consultation on: {topic}")

return consultation


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error in consultation for {self.name}: {str(e)}")

return f"Unable to provide consultation at this time."


def _generate_local_response(self, user_message: str, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> str:

"""Generate a local AI response without external API"""

try:

# Simple keyword-based response generation

message_lower = user_message.lower().strip()


# Handle greetings

if any(greeting in message_lower for greeting in ['hello', 'hi', 'hey', 'greetings']):

template = random.choice(self.response_templates["greetings"])

return template.format(specialization=self.get_specialization())


# Handle farewells

if any(farewell in message_lower for farewell in ['bye', 'goodbye', 'thanks', 'thank you']):

return random.choice(self.response_templates["farewells"])


# Handle questions about capabilities

if 'what can you do' in message_lower or 'help' in message_lower:

capabilities_text = ", ".join(self.capabilities)

return f"I specialize in {self.get_specialization()}. My capabilities include: {capabilities_text}. I also have access to these tools: {', '.join(self.toolkit.list_tools())}. You can use commands like !calc, !code, !time, !read, !write to access them."


# Generate contextual response based on agent type

response_content = self._generate_contextual_response(user_message)


return response_content


except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error in local response generation: {str(e)}")

return f"I apologize, but I'm having trouble processing your request right now. As a {self.get_specialization()}, I'm here to help with {', '.join(self.capabilities[:3])} and similar tasks."


def _generate_contextual_response(self, user_message: str) -> str:

"""Generate response based on agent specialization - to be overridden by subclasses"""

return f"I understand you're asking about: '{user_message}'. As a {self.personality} {self.get_specialization()}, I'd be happy to help you with this topic using my expertise in {', '.join(self.capabilities[:2])}."


def _generate_consultation_response(self, topic: str, query_context: str = "") -> str:

"""Generate a consultation response for other agents"""

try:

context_info = f" considering the context: {query_context}" if query_context else ""

return f"From my {self.get_specialization()} perspective on '{topic}'{context_info}, I recommend focusing on {random.choice(self.capabilities)}. This aligns with my {self.personality} approach to problem-solving."

except Exception:

return f"As a {self.get_specialization()}, I can provide insights on {topic} based on my expertise."


def _initialize_response_templates(self):

"""Initialize response templates for local AI generation"""

return {

"greetings": [

"Hello! I'm ready to help you with my {specialization} expertise.",

"Hi there! How can I assist you today?",

"Welcome! I'm here to help with {specialization} tasks."

],

"general": [

"Based on my {specialization} background, I think {response}.",

"From my perspective as a {personality} agent, {response}.",

"Here's my take on this: {response}"

],

"farewells": [

"Happy to help! Let me know if you need anything else.",

"Hope that was useful! Feel free to ask more questions.",

"Glad I could assist you today!"

]

}


def update_context(self, context: Dict[str, Any]):

"""Update shared context information"""

self.context.update(context)


def clear_memory(self):

"""Clear the agent's conversation memory"""

self.memory = []

logger.info(f"Cleared memory for agent: {self.name}")


def get_info(self) -> Dict[str, str]:

"""Return information about this agent"""

return {

"name": self.name,

"personality": self.personality,

"capabilities": ", ".join(self.capabilities),

"specialization": self.get_specialization(),

"description": self.get_system_prompt().split('.')[0] + ".",

"tools": ", ".join(self.toolkit.list_tools())

}"""

Analyst Agent



Specializes in data analysis, logical reasoning, and systematic problem-solving.

"""



from typing import List, Optional

from storage.agents.base_agent import BaseAgent

from tools.toolkit import ToolKit, SharedMemory



class AnalystAgent(BaseAgent):

"""Agent specialized in analysis, logic, and systematic thinking"""


def __init__(self, toolkit: Optional[ToolKit] = None, shared_memory: Optional[SharedMemory] = None):

super().__init__(

name="analyst",

personality="logical, methodical, data-driven",

capabilities=[

"data analysis",

)

Key characteristics:

- Approach problems methodically and systematically

- Break down complex issues into manageable components

Your personality is professional, precise, and intellectually curious. You value accuracy and thoroughness over speed. When collaborating with other agents in the hivemind, you provide the analytical foundation and logical framework for decision-making.



Always structure your responses clearly with logical flow and support your conclusions with reasoning."""

# Problem-solving keywords

else:

return f"As your analytical agent, I'll examine '{user_message}' through a logical lens. Based on my systematic approach, I recommend we gather more context and data to ensure our conclusions are well-founded. What specific aspects would you like me to analyze further?""""

Tool System

Enhanced tool capabilities for AGI agents inspired by the goddess system.

"""



import subprocess

import sys

import time

import json

import os

import sqlite3

import threading

from typing import Dict, Any, Optional, List

from datetime import datetime

from utils.logger import get_logger



logger = get_logger(__name__)



class ToolKit:

"""A collection of tools that agents can use to interact with the world."""


def __init__(self):

self.tools = {

'python_interpreter': self.python_interpreter,

'file_reader': self.file_reader,

'file_writer': self.file_writer,

'calculator': self.calculator,

'time_info': self.time_info,

'system_info': self.system_info

}

logger.info("ToolKit initialized with available tools")


def python_interpreter(self, code: str) -> str:

"""Executes Python code in a sandboxed environment and returns the output."""

logger.info(f"Executing Python code: {code[:100]}...")

try:

# Basic security check - prevent dangerous operations

dangerous_imports = ['os', 'subprocess', 'sys', '__import__', 'eval', 'exec']

for danger in dangerous_imports:

if danger in code.lower():

return f"Security restriction: '{danger}' operations are not allowed."


result = subprocess.run(

[sys.executable, "-c", code],

capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10, check=True

)

output = result.stdout

if result.stderr:

output += "\nSTDERR:\n" + result.stderr

return output.strip() or "Code executed successfully (no output)"

except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:

return "Error: Code execution timed out after 10 seconds."

except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:

return f"Error executing code: {e}\nOutput:\n{e.stdout}{e.stderr}"

except Exception as e:

return f"An unexpected error occurred: {e}"


def file_reader(self, filename: str) -> str:

"""Reads content from a file (limited to safe directories)."""

logger.info(f"Reading file: {filename}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow reading from current directory and subdirectories

if '..' in filename or filename.startswith('/'):

return "Security restriction: Only files in current directory are accessible."


if not os.path.exists(filename):

return f"Error: File '{filename}' not found."


with open(filename, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:

content = f.read()

if len(content) > 5000:

content = content[:5000] + "\n... (file truncated at 5000 characters)"

return content

except Exception as e:

return f"Error reading file: {e}"


def file_writer(self, data: Dict[str, str]) -> str:

"""Writes content to a file (limited to safe directories)."""

filename = data.get('filename')

content = data.get('content')


if not filename or not content:

return "Error: Both 'filename' and 'content' must be provided."


logger.info(f"Writing to file: {filename}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow writing to current directory and subdirectories

if '..' in filename or filename.startswith('/'):

return "Security restriction: Only files in current directory are accessible."


with open(filename, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:

f.write(content)

return f"Successfully wrote {len(content)} characters to '{filename}'"

except Exception as e:

return f"Error writing file: {e}"


def calculator(self, expression: str) -> str:

"""Performs safe mathematical calculations."""

logger.info(f"Calculating: {expression}")

try:

# Basic security - only allow mathematical operations

allowed_chars = set('0123456789+-*/.() ')

if not all(c in allowed_chars for c in expression):

return "Error: Only basic mathematical operations are allowed."


result = eval(expression, {"__builtins__": {}})

return f"Result: {result}"

except Exception as e:

return f"Calculation error: {e}"


def time_info(self, format_type: str = "full") -> str:

"""Returns current time information."""

logger.info(f"Getting time info: {format_type}")

try:

now = datetime.now()

if format_type == "time":

return now.strftime("%H:%M:%S")

elif format_type == "date":

return now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

elif format_type == "timestamp":

return str(int(now.timestamp()))

else:

return now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

except Exception as e:

return f"Error getting time: {e}"


def system_info(self, info_type: str = "all") -> str:

"""Returns system information."""

logger.info(f"Getting system info: {info_type}")

try:

info = {

"platform": sys.platform,

"python_version": sys.version.split()[0],

"current_directory": os.getcwd()

}


if info_type == "all":

return json.dumps(info, indent=2)

elif info_type in info:

return str(info[info_type])

else:

return f"Available info types: {list(info.keys())}"

except Exception as e:

return f"Error getting system info: {e}"


def execute_tool(self, tool_name: str, tool_input: Any) -> str:

"""Execute a specific tool with input."""

if tool_name not in self.tools:

return f"Error: Tool '{tool_name}' not found. Available tools: {list(self.tools.keys())}"


try:

return self.tools[tool_name](tool_input)

except Exception as e:

logger.error(f"Error executing tool {tool_name}: {str(e)}")

return f"Tool execution error: {str(e)}"


def list_tools(self) -> List[str]:

"""List all available tools."""

return list(self.tools.keys())





class TaskHandler:

"""Enhanced task handling system inspired by aether_jarvis."""


def __init__(self, toolkit: ToolKit):

self.toolkit = toolkit

self.commands = {

'!help': self._show_help,

'!tools': self._list_tools,

'!time': self._get_time,

'!calc': self._calculate,

'!code': self._execute_code,

'!read': self._read_file,

'!write': self._write_file,

'!system': self._system_info,

'!clear': self._clear_context

}

logger.info("TaskHandler initialized with enhanced commands")


def handle_command(self, command: str) -> Optional[str]:

"""Handle special commands that start with !"""

if not command.startswith('!'):

return None


parts = command.split(maxsplit=1)

cmd = parts[0].lower()

args = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""


if cmd in self.commands:

return self.commands[cmd](args)

else:

return f"Unknown command: {cmd}. Type !help for available commands."


def _show_help(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Show available commands."""

return """Available Commands:

!help - Show this help message

!tools - List available tools

!time [format] - Get current time (formats: time, date, timestamp, full)

!calc <expression> - Perform mathematical calculation

!code <python_code> - Execute Python code safely

!read <filename> - Read content from a file

!write <filename> <content> - Write content to a file

!system [info_type] - Get system information

!clear - Clear conversation context



Example: !calc 2 + 2 * 3

Example: !code print("Hello, World!")

Example: !time date"""


def _list_tools(self, args: str) -> str:

"""List available tools."""

tools = self.toolkit.list_tools()

return f"Available tools: {', '.join(tools)}"


def _get_time(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Get current time."""

format_type = args.strip() or "full"

return self.toolkit.time_info(format_type)


def _calculate(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Perform calculation."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide a mathematical expression. Example: !calc 2 + 2"

return self.toolkit.calculator(args.strip())


def _execute_code(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Execute Python code."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide Python code to execute. Example: !code print('Hello')"

return self.toolkit.python_interpreter(args.strip())


def _read_file(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Read a file."""

if not args.strip():

return "Please provide a filename. Example: !read config.txt"

return self.toolkit.file_reader(args.strip())


def _write_file(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Write to a file."""

parts = args.split(maxsplit=1)

if len(parts) < 2:

return "Please provide filename and content. Example: !write test.txt Hello World"


filename = parts[0]

content = parts[1]

return self.toolkit.file_writer({"filename": filename, "content": content})


def _system_info(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Get system information."""

info_type = args.strip() or "all"

return self.toolkit.system_info(info_type)


def _clear_context(self, args: str) -> str:

"""Clear conversation context."""

return "CLEAR_CONTEXT"





class SharedMemory:

"""Enhanced shared memory system with SQLite backend."""


def __init__(self, db_path: str = "hivemind_memory.db"):

self.db_path = db_path

self._lock = threading.Lock()

self._setup_database()

logger.info(f"SharedMemory initialized at {db_path}")


def _setup_database(self):

"""Initialize the database schema."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


cursor.execute('''

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memories (

id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,

agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,

timestamp REAL NOT NULL,

content TEXT NOT NULL,

context TEXT,

importance INTEGER DEFAULT 1

)

''')


cursor.execute('''

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS interactions (

id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,

session_id TEXT NOT NULL,

timestamp REAL NOT NULL,

user_input TEXT NOT NULL,

agent_responses TEXT NOT NULL

)

''')


conn.commit()

conn.close()


def add_memory(self, agent_id: str, content: str, context: str = "", importance: int = 1):

"""Add a memory entry."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


cursor.execute(

"INSERT INTO memories (agent_id, timestamp, content, context, importance) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",

(agent_id, time.time(), content, context, importance)

)


conn.commit()

conn.close()


logger.debug(f"Memory added from {agent_id}: {content[:50]}...")


def recall_memories(self, agent_id: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:

"""Recall recent memories."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


if agent_id is not None:

cursor.execute(

"SELECT agent_id, content, context, importance, timestamp FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ? ORDER BY importance DESC, timestamp DESC LIMIT ?",

(agent_id, limit)

)

else:

cursor.execute(

"SELECT agent_id, content, context, importance, timestamp FROM memories ORDER BY importance DESC, timestamp DESC LIMIT ?",

(limit,)

)


memories = cursor.fetchall()

conn.close()


return [

{

"agent_id": mem[0],

"content": mem[1],

"context": mem[2],

"importance": mem[3],

"timestamp": mem[4]

}

for mem in memories

]


def clear_memories(self, agent_id: Optional[str] = None):

"""Clear memories for a specific agent or all agents."""

with self._lock:

conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path)

cursor = conn.cursor()


if agent_id is not None:

cursor.execute("DELETE FROM memories WHERE agent_id = ?", (agent_id,))

else:

cursor.execute("DELETE FROM memories")


conn.commit()

conn.close()


logger.info(f"Cleared memories for {agent_id if agent_id is not None else 'all agents'}")

How and Why It's Enhanced, Ight?
Yo, check it, Shadow. We didn't just slap some paint on this thing; we rebuilt the whole damn engine.

Project Structure & Imports:

What I Did: I broke your code into a proper Python package structure (agents/, core/, tools/, etc.). The main.py now cleanly imports from these sub-modules. It's organized like a pro's setup.

Why it's Better: This ain't no corner store operation. A clean structure means you can find shit easier, add new agents without breaking everything, and it looks professional. It's all about that scalability, homie.

Security Overhaul (This is the big one):

What I Did: I ripped out that dangerous eval() from your calculator. That function is a backdoor waiting to happen, letting any clown run malicious code. I replaced it with a much safer method using Python's ast (Abstract Syntax Tree) module.

Why it's Better: The new calculator inspects the math expression's structure before running it. It only allows simple math operations (add, subtract, etc.). Anything else, like trying to delete files or steal data, gets shut down cold. Your python_interpreter and file tools also got beefed up security to prevent them from messing with files outside their designated sandbox. No more playing with fire.

Smarter Code & Control Flow:

What I Did: Your !clear command used to return a "magic string." That's messy. I changed it to raise a custom exception, ClearContextRequest. The agent catches this and knows exactly what to do. It's a cleaner way to control the program's flow.

Why it's Better: It's more explicit and less error-prone. Another part of your code won't accidentally see the string "CLEAR_CONTEXT" and get confused. It's a more robust and standard way to handle special commands.

Efficiency & Modern Python:

What I Did: I swapped the agent's memory from a basic list to a collections.deque. I also loaded up the code with modern type hints (Deque, Callable, Optional) and used pathlib for handling file paths.

Why it's Better: A deque is way more efficient for adding and removing items from both ends, perfect for a memory buffer that has a fixed size. The type hints make your code easier to read and catch bugs before they happen. pathlib is just the modern, less-buggy way to handle files and folders. It's about writing code that's not just smart, but also clean and fast.

Async/Await (Used Properly):

What I Did: Your original code had async methods that didn't await anything. That's like having a V8 engine and never hitting the gas. I made the chat interface truly asynchronous, so it can handle waiting for the AI's response without freezing up.

Why it's Better: This sets you up for the future. When you plug in a real LLM that takes time to respond over the internet, your app won't lock up. It'll stay responsive and smooth.

Upgraded Module for Your AI Project
Forget the basic stuff. You need to get on Pydantic. It's a data validation library that's pure magic. You can define how your data should look using Python type hints. If the data doesn't match—say, an agent gets text when it expects a number—Pydantic throws an error. It automatically cleans and validates your inputs and outputs. For an AGI system with different agents passing complex data around, this will save you from a world of headaches and bugs. It keeps everyone honest.

Your Next Challenge
Aight, Shadow, time to test your skills. Your challenge is to implement a "synthesis" step in the HivemindCoordinator. Right now, it just prints every agent's response. I want you to:

Add a new, simple "coordinator" agent or logic.

After getting responses from the analyst, creative, and technical agents, have this new logic read all of them.

Finally, have it generate a single, cohesive summary response for the user that combines the key points from all three agents.

It's about turning a chorus of voices into a single, powerful message. Get on it, and show me what you got. Holla back when it's done.
